hypothesis: If Bayesian optimization guided by pretrained polymer representations is coupled with a fragment-based polyamine generator and accurate two-stage MD/GCMC simulations using DFT-refined interaction parameters, then high-CO2-uptake polyamines at 298 K and 0.4 mbar will be identified in significantly fewer evaluations than random or uninformed search because the refined physics reduces simulation error and the surrogate exploits structure–property correlations to guide sampling.
